name: Andrea's Fudge Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Position rack in center of oven and preheat to 350°F.
2. Lightly butter a 9-inch springform pan. Dust the pan with flour and tap out excess.
3. In the top of a double boiler over hot, not simmering water, melt together espresso and chocolate, stirring occasionally.
4. Remove the pan from heat and let cool until tepid.
5. In a large bowl using a hand-held electric mixer set at medium-high speed, cream sugar and butter together until light and fluffy.
6. Add egg yolks one at a time to the mixture, beating well after each addition.
7. Beat in flour.
8. In a large grease-free bowl, using a hand-held mixer at medium-high speed, beat egg whites until they form stiff, shiny peaks.
9. Fold 1/4 of the whites into the chocolate mixture to lighten it.
10. Then fold in the remaining whites.
11. Fold in butter and flour mixture.
12. Scrape the batter into the prepared pan and bake for 60 to 70 minutes or until the top is crusty and cracked and the middle is still slightly moist.
13. Remove the cake from the oven and let it cool completely on a wire rack.
14. Remove the sides of the springform pan and transfer the cake to a serving plate.
15. If desired, place a doily on top of the cake and sprinkle with confectioners sugar. Remove the doily to form a pattern.
